title: resolveAgeBracket null-birthYear fallback returns 'adult' instead of 'adolescent'
body_excerpt: The PR sweeps every null-birthYear fallback to 'adolescent' for defence-in-depth safety. This function feeds both getAgeVoice() and getSessionTypeGuidance() for LLM exchanges — the most safety-c
evidence: apps/api/src/services/exchange-prompts.ts:32 — the function signature was changed from `(birthYear?: number | null): AgeBracket` to `(birthYear: number): AgeBracket` (non-nullable). A comment at line 27–31 explains: 'The previous nullable signature here silently routed unknown ages to TEEN_VOICE… Tightening the type makes that trap unrepresentable.' The null-default-to-adult path is gone because null is no longer an accepted input; callers must provide a valid birthYear guaranteed non-null by the DB schema.

title: docs/project_context.md frontmatter project_name case change may break tooling
body_excerpt: The field changed from 'eduagent' (lowercase) to 'MentoMate' (mixed-case). Archon scripts (e.g. .archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh) parse this frontmatter to identify the project. If any script,
evidence: Grepped .archon/scripts/ and _bmad/ for 'project_name', 'eduagent' (as a case-sensitive key), and 'MentoMate'. No script in .archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh references 'project_name' at all; .archon/scripts/cleanup-compare-runs.sh hard-codes 'eduagent-build' as a path segment (not matched against the frontmatter field). No tooling does a case-sensitive match on the frontmatter value. The risk the CCR flagged is theoretical and the grep confirms no actual breakage.

title: buildLegacyEmailIdempotencyKey first parameter not narrowed to 'weekly'|'monthly'
body_excerpt: The function comment says "Do not use for new email types," but the type system will not enforce this. A future developer calling buildLegacyEmailIdempotencyKey("feedback", ...) gets no compile-time
evidence: apps/api/src/services/dedupe-key.ts:34 — the function signature now reads `prefix: 'weekly' | 'monthly'`. The type was narrowed exactly as suggested. Callers in monthly-report-cron.ts:475 and weekly-progress-push.ts:718 both pass 'monthly'/'weekly' respectively.

title: Adding 'progress' noun may misclassify content questions as app-help
body_excerpt: Adding `progress` to the noun list introduces a boundary case: "Where can I see progress on this topic?" or "Where can I see my progress in the textbook?" would now be classified as app-help (rout
evidence: apps/api/src/services/app-help-map.ts:45 — 'progress' is restricted to `progress (tab|page|screen|section)` within APP_HELP_SPECIFIC, not a bare noun. apps/api/src/services/app-help-map.test.ts:246–251 — negative test cases 'Where can I see progress on this topic?' and 'Where can I see my progress in the textbook?' are present and assert `isAppHelpQuery()` returns false.

title: ConsentManagementSection silently disappears on query error
body_excerpt: ConsentManagementSection silently disappears on query error. When consent.isError is true (network failure, server error, 403), hasConsentRecord resolves to false (loading=false, consentStatus=null)
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/child/[profileId]/index.tsx:433–436 — `hasConsentRecord` now includes `consent.isError ||` so the section stays mounted on error. Lines 509–528 show a dedicated error UI block rendered when `consent.isError` is true, with an error banner (testID='consent-status-error') and a retry button (testID='consent-status-retry'). The fix satisfies the UX Resilience rule.

title: Plural regression in deletionGraceDays — missing _one/_other variants in all 7 locale files
body_excerpt: Plural regression in `deletionGraceDays` (all 7 locale files) — see inline comment on `en.json`. The key passes `count` but lacks `_one`/`_other` variants; i18next will render "1 days left to cance
evidence: All 7 locale files (en.json:1955, de.json:1884, es.json:1884, ja.json:1884, nb.json:1884, pl.json:1909, pt.json:1909) still have a single `deletionGraceDays` key without `_one`/`_other` suffixes. For example, de.json:1884 still reads `"deletionGraceDays": "{{count}} Tage verbleiben…"` — German will render '1 Tage' for the last day. The i18next plural suffix pattern used everywhere else (childCount_one/childCount_other etc.) is not applied here.
suggested_notion_title: i18n: deletionGraceDays missing _one/_other plural variants in all 7 locales
suggested_notion_body: All 7 locale files (en, de, es, ja, nb, pl, pt) have `deletionGraceDays` as a single key without `_one`/`_other` variants. i18next will render '1 days left to cancel deletion.' for the last day in English, and grammatically wrong singular forms in every other locale (e.g., German '1 Tage' instead of '1 Tag'). Fix: add `deletionGraceDays_one` and `deletionGraceDays_other` to all 7 locale files following the project's plural convention.

title: [Medium] J04 test name contradicts what is tested
body_excerpt: The test is titled "parent taps child card to open child progress" but after this PR it clicks the Progress *action button*, not the card. Tapping the card (`parent-home-check-child-` Pressable) rou
evidence: apps/mobile/e2e-web/flows/journeys/j04-parent-inline-learn.spec.ts:5 — test is now titled 'J-04 parent opens child progress detail from child action', which correctly describes clicking the `parent-home-child-progress-${childId}` action button (line 24) rather than the card. Also line 13–14 adds explicit `toBeVisible()` on the childCard before resolving the child ID, partially addressing the asymmetric assertion concern.

title: .test-receipts/mobile.json records non-standard command — attestation gap
body_excerpt: `.test-receipts/mobile.json` records a command that does not match what `scripts/record-test-receipt.sh` writes. That script hard-codes `TEST_CMD='pnpm exec nx run mobile:test'` and always records t
evidence: The test receipt system (`scripts/record-test-receipt.sh`, `scripts/verify-test-receipts.sh`, `.test-receipts/` directory) no longer exists in the codebase. The pre-push gate has been replaced by `.husky/pre-push` → `scripts/pre-push-tests.sh` which uses `SKIP_PRE_PUSH` as its bypass. The finding is moot because the receipt infrastructure was removed entirely.

title: All non-EN locales have English strings for familySummary
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/mobile/src/i18n/locales/de.json` (and `es.json`, `ja.json`, `nb.json`, `pl.json`, `pt.json`)
Line: new `familySummary` block
Issue: All non-EN locales have English strings for `familySum
evidence: Compared apps/mobile/src/i18n/locales at home.parent.familySummary for all 6 non-EN locales. None of the 22 keys in any non-EN locale matches the English strings. For example, de.json:158–179 shows fully translated German strings: 'withSessions_one: "…{{count}} Sitzung diese Woche"', 'parentExampleNudge: "Zeig, wie es geht: starte eine kurze Sitzung."', etc.

title: suggestionsErrorStatus derived from raw HTTP status codes in pick-book screen
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/pick-book/[subjectId].tsx`
Line: ~177–184
Issue: `suggestionsErrorStatus` is derived by directly inspecting `suggestionsQuery.error.status` and then hard-coding HT
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/pick-book/[subjectId].tsx:171–176 — the code now calls `classifyApiError(suggestionsQuery.error)` and uses `suggestionsError.blocksManualEntry` (a field added to the FormattedApiError interface in apps/mobile/src/lib/format-api-error.ts:278). No raw `.status` integer parsing exists in the vicinity. The `blocksManualEntry` field is computed in `format-api-error.ts:281–293` by `blocksManualEntryForCategory()` which maps category names (not HTTP codes) to boolean.

title: getTableSection regex captures empty string — CFG-13 tests always throw
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/api/src/wrangler-config.test.ts`
Line: L278–287
Issue: `getTableSection` regex `(?=^\[\n$)` with the `m` flag causes `$` to match at end of the section header line, so `section[1]` is
evidence: apps/api/src/wrangler-config.test.ts:24–37 — the regex `^\[${escaped}\]([\s\S]*?)(?=^\[|$(?![\r\n]))` with flag 'm' is present. Ran the regex against the actual wrangler.toml content: section[1] for 'env.staging.vars' captured 173 non-empty characters of TOML content (truthy). The original CCR finding's claim that section[1] is always empty is incorrect — the `$(?![\r\n])` lookahead prevents matching end-of-line, only end-of-file. The function works correctly.

title: SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1 used to bypass mobile receipt gate on non-revert PR
body_excerpt:
File: PR description
Line: —
Issue: `SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1` was used to bypass the mobile receipt gate on a fix PR. CLAUDE.md limits this bypass to revert PRs only.
evidence: The test receipt system (`scripts/record-test-receipt.sh`, `scripts/verify-test-receipts.sh`, `SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK`) no longer exists in the codebase. Only .claude/worktrees/ carries a copy (legacy/unreachable). The pre-push gate is now `scripts/pre-push-tests.sh` with `SKIP_PRE_PUSH` as bypass. The bypass cited in the PR was a one-time procedural issue on a since-replaced system.

title: SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1 used to bypass mobile pre-push receipt gate (duplicate finding)
body_excerpt:
File: PR description
Line: —
Issue: `SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1` was used to bypass the mobile pre-push receipt gate. CLAUDE.md restricts this bypass to revert PRs only. This PR is a regular fix, so th
evidence: Duplicate of the other PR #290 finding. Same evidence applies: the test receipt system (record-test-receipt.sh, verify-test-receipts.sh, SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK) has been removed entirely from the codebase and replaced with pre-push-tests.sh / SKIP_PRE_PUSH. The procedural bypass on the now-defunct system is moot.

title: GC6 triggered: internal mock deferral not recorded in commit message
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress.test.tsx`
Line: —
Issue: GC6 triggered: test file was edited, but pre-existing internal mock warnings were not swept. PR description acknowledges them but G
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress.test.tsx still contains 5 internal jest.mock calls (lines 233, 258, 268, 275, 280). Git log for commit 5c789d124 (the PR #296 merge commit) shows no 'GC6 defer:' line in the commit message. The most recent GC6 cleanup wave (commit 39e6ff415) did not include progress.test.tsx. The deferral was noted in PR description only, which CLAUDE.md GC6 explicitly disallows — requires commit message with file paths + count.
suggested_notion_title: GC6: progress.test.tsx — 5 internal mock sites need commit-message deferral or cleanup
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress.test.tsx has 5 internal jest.mock calls (lines 233, 258, 268, 275, 280). GC6 was triggered when the file was edited in PR #296 but the deferral was only noted in the PR description, not the commit message. CLAUDE.md GC6 requires the deferral to be in the commit message with file path + count. Either run /my:mockfix to sweep the file, or amend the commit to add 'GC6 defer: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress.test.tsx — 5 internal mock sites'.

title: GC6 deferral incomplete — missing count of legacy mock sites
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/progress.test.tsx`
Line: —
Issue: GC6 deferral is incomplete. The PR description acknowledges "existing internal mock warnings in progress.test.tsx" but omits the r
evidence: Same as the companion PR #296 finding. progress.test.tsx:233,258,268,275,280 — 5 internal jest.mock calls still present. No GC6 defer entry with count in any commit message touching this file. This is a duplicate finding but both are UNADDRESSED for the same reason.

title: emitJsonErrorFrame called unconditionally after onError — double SSE error frame
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/api/src/services/streaming/sse-utf8.ts`
Line: L59–68
Issue: When `onError` is provided and succeeds, `emitJsonErrorFrame` is still called unconditionally after it. A caller whose `onErro
evidence: apps/api/src/route-utils/sse-utf8.ts:65–72 — the pattern remains: if onError is provided, it is called inside a try/catch, but `emitJsonErrorFrame(stream)` is always called at line 72 unconditionally regardless of whether onError ran. No current caller passes onError (sessions.ts calls use only 2 args), so no user-visible bug today. However the API contract is broken: any future caller that provides onError to emit custom error content will get a double SSE frame.
suggested_notion_title: API: streamSSEUtf8 emits double error frame when onError is provided
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/route-utils/sse-utf8.ts:72 — `emitJsonErrorFrame` is called unconditionally after `onError`, causing two SSE `error` frames if onError succeeds. No current caller passes onError so no user-visible bug today, but the contract is broken. Fix: only call `emitJsonErrorFrame` when `onError` is absent or when `onError` itself throws. Move line 72 inside the `catch (onErrorCaught)` block and add it to the no-onError branch.

title: generateBookTopics fallback logic (captureException + buildFallbackBookTopics) in route handler
body_excerpt:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/books.ts`
Line: ~L127–L149
Issue: The try/catch fallback for `generateBookTopics` (including the `captureException` call and the `buildFallbackBookTopics` selection) is b
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/books.ts:154–175 and 201–218 — two identical inline try/catch blocks each call generateBookTopics, catch errors with captureException, then call buildFallbackBookTopics directly inside the route handler. No `generateBookTopicsWithFallback` service function exists (grepped entire apps/api/src with no match). The business logic is still duplicated in the route handler, violating CLAUDE.md 'Business logic belongs in services/, not route handlers'.
suggested_notion_title: API: generateBookTopics fallback logic duplicated in route handler — extract to service
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/routes/books.ts:154–175 and 201–218 have identical inline try/catch blocks calling generateBookTopics + captureException + buildFallbackBookTopics inside the route handler. CLAUDE.md requires business logic in services/. Extract to a `generateBookTopicsWithFallback(title, description, learnerAge, priorKnowledge)` service function in apps/api/src/services/book-generation.ts (or a sibling file). Both call sites in books.ts reduce to a single await call.

title: Temporary regex probe scripts (test-pattern.js, test-pattern2.js, test-pattern3.js) committed to repo root
body_excerpt:
File: `test-pattern.js`, `test-pattern2.js`, `test-pattern3.js`
Line: n/a
Issue: Temporary regex probe scripts committed to the repo root. They include hardcoded developer-specific Windows paths and
evidence: test-pattern.js, test-pattern2.js, and test-pattern3.js do not exist in the working tree. Git log shows commit 391ddbaec 'chore(api): remove scratch pattern scripts' after commit 5db1ddf93 which introduced them in PR #315. The files were cleaned up in a follow-on commit.
